skill.name: wiki-researcher
skill.description: Guides deep, evidence-based codebase research across architecture, data flow, dependencies, patterns, and recommendations.
skill.summary: Investigates codebases through structured, evidence-backed research.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators, but review found only instructional Markdown text. No executable scripts, network calls, data exfiltration logic, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static finding at line 61 points to an inline citation format example. It does not execute Ruby, shell commands, or any external process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is Markdown instruction text only. No executable code, command runner, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static finding at line 3 appears to match ordinary prose in the skill description. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or hashing logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is YAML front matter describing research behavior. There is no code path that could invoke a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Data Flow Evidence Rule Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static finding at line 35 points to a documentation rule about tracing data flow. It does not reference encryption, hashing, or security primitives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a Markdown evidence table for code research. No weak cryptographic operation or implementation exists at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Pattern Text Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static finding at line 47 matches analysis guidance about design patterns and trade-offs. It contains no cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a numbered research process. It is not executable and does not configure any cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Diagram Guidance Misread as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static finding at line 63 asks for Mermaid diagrams when helpful. It does not instruct system discovery, host enumeration, or sensitive environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction concerns presentation of architecture findings. No commands, filesystem probes, or host reconnaissance steps are described.

content.value_statement: Large codebases are difficult to understand when answers rely on assumptions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured research grounded in file-level evidence.
actual_capabilities: Structures codebase investigation into architecture, data flow, dependencies, patterns, and recommendations.
Requires claims to include file paths, functions, call chains, or other traceable evidence.
Separates verified facts from inferred conclusions during technical analysis.
Prompts the assistant to identify unexplored areas and open questions.
Encourages Mermaid diagrams when they clarify architecture or data movement.
limitations: It does not run analysis tools or inspect files by itself.
It depends on the assistant having access to the relevant repository files.
It may produce long answers for broad research topics.
It does not replace security testing, profiling, or formal code review.

use_cases.title: Map a New Service
use_cases.description: Trace entry points, core modules, and main data paths before making changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Notes
use_cases.description: Turn repository evidence into clear documentation for onboarding or design review.

use_cases.title: Investigate Risky Code Areas
use_cases.description: Identify coupling, unclear ownership, and technical debt with supporting file references.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Repository Overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when joining a project or reviewing an unfamiliar repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-researcher to explain how this repository is organized. Ground every major claim in file paths and note what remains untraced.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Flow Trace
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need to understand one feature end to end.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-researcher to trace how the selected feature works from entry point to final output. Separate confirmed facts from inferences.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Dependency Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before a refactor or migration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-researcher to analyze the architecture, module boundaries, and dependency chains for this subsystem. Include evidence and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Technical Debt Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for planning remediation work across a complex system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-researcher to perform a five-lens investigation of this system. Cover architecture, data flow, integrations, patterns, risks, and recommendations.

output_examples.input: Explain how authentication works in this service.
output_examples.output: A structured investigation that identifies entry points, traces request flow, lists supporting files, and marks unresolved questions.

output_examples.input: Find the main dependencies between billing and account management.
output_examples.output: A dependency-focused summary with verified module relationships, coupling concerns, and references to the files that support each claim.

output_examples.input: Document the data flow for order creation.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step flow narrative with transformations, storage points, integrations, and confidence ratings for each major finding.

best_practices: Ask about one system, feature, or workflow at a time.
Provide repository access and name the files or directories that matter most.
Request confidence ratings and open questions for complex conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for quick answers that do not need evidence.
Do not ask it to confirm an assumption without allowing repository review.
Do not treat diagrams as authoritative unless each connection is traced.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant research codebases deeply and support findings with concrete repository evidence.

faq.question: Does it execute code or shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill file only contains guidance for research and reporting.

faq.question: Can it create architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It encourages Mermaid diagrams when they clarify architecture, dependencies, or data flow.

faq.question: Is it useful for onboarding?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide repository walkthroughs that distinguish verified facts from assumptions.

faq.question: Can it replace a code review?
faq.answer: No. It supports investigation and documentation, but it does not replace review, tests, or security assessment.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
